required_operator_inputs: treat kExplainoAxisRegistry as the single common-axis authority for canonical explaino_all
repair canonical explaino_all so active registry axes stay on the full common-axis runtime path instead of rewriting to a partial owner lane
replace OS-mouse live viewer tests with in-process set-value automation that cannot steal the operator mouse
preserve Color Pipeline smooth-escape ramp numeric live edits, draft-to-live behavior, row click automation, and balance_void_grade runtime-backed behavior
preserve additive schema visibility on explaino_all plus registry owner lanes
repair the native test command harness so crashed test executables cannot report a false green
repair the Color Pipeline schedule bridge so shipped advanced smooth-escape grading rows import back to live lanes instead of zero-lane snapshots
preserve explicit selector identity, smooth_escape fast-path behavior, and SampleFractalPoints legacy projection surface
forbidden_operator_prompts: hardcode a Python seven-name subset as the authority instead of consuming the C++ registry
make explaino_all neutral or remove its shared axis controls
rewrite explaino_all into a partial owner lane before the canonical all-axis runtime branch can run
claim live slider repair from headless runtime proof alone
leave first-party runtime tests using SetCursorPos, mouse_event, MOUSEEVENTF, SendInput, or drag_screen_rect
rebreak Color Pipeline live numeric edits or existing in-process click automation
claim native Color Pipeline validation green when a test executable exits by access violation or another negative Windows process status
change SampleFractalPoints from the shipped legacy projection surface
widen this slice into Meta-Basin, Operator-Itinerary, DSL/program-space, generic color expansion, or a broad UI/engine rewrite
close without focused native/runtime proof, hostile audit, checkpoint, receipts, push, and truthful closeout
